frameworks.name: natural scene statistics hypothesis
frameworks.one_line: low-level tuning in category-selective cortex reflects features diagnostic of preferred categories

frameworks.name: voxelwise forward encoding model
frameworks.one_line: estimates per-voxel feature and spatial selectivity from fMRI responses to natural images

frameworks.name: Gabor feature representation
frameworks.one_line: orientation and spatial frequency basis used to characterize low-level image content

core_question: Do low-level feature and spatial tuning biases in category-selective visual cortex reflect the statistical informativeness of those features for the region's preferred semantic category?
key_assumptions: Gabor features capture low-level image content relevant to cortical tuning
voxelwise encoding can dissociate low-level feature selectivity from category selectivity
natural scene image statistics shape feature-category associations
manipulations: rich natural scene images sampled across semantic categories
voxel-level selectivity estimated across visual cortex regions
key_findings: low-level Gabor features reliably associate with high-level semantic categories in natural scenes
category-selective voxels show systematic low-level feature and spatial selectivity biases consistent with their preferred category
these low-level tuning biases are not driven by category selectivity itself
interpretations: low-level tuning in higher visual cortex reflects the diagnosticity of features for semantic categories
natural scene statistics provide a general account of feature-category coupling in visual cortex
